title: vendor: update docker v28.3.0 and c/{common,image,storage} to main
body:
<!--

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.

Fixes: #26081

comments.body:
```
bin/podman           size= 61497432  delta=-3690952
bin/podman-remote    size= 48297168  delta=-3705984
bin/podmansh         size=        6  delta=     0
bin/podman-testing   size= 50360152  delta=-2959624
bin/quadlet          size=  3453424  delta=     0
bin/rootlessport     size=  4590582  delta=     0
```

Now these are some nice deltas.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

Line count is not a good metric alone, the real nice part (besides binary size) it that we loose like 16 dependencies that we need to track/update in case of CVE's, point in case https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26535
